missing_info: Current live interface status for the two interfaces (to validate the alert): full output of 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' on R2 and on R3 (include admin/oper state, line protocol, last input/output, input/output errors, CRC, encapsulation, DTE/DCE, clock rate if present).

Running-config for the interfaces: 'show running-config interface Serial0/1/1' on R2 and R3 (to confirm whether the interface is administratively up/down or has an explicit shutdown command or recent config change).
Recent system/log messages around the time the alert was raised: 'show logging' or last 200 log lines from R2 and R3 (to capture any link up/down events, clocking changes, or manual admin changes).

Peer-side state for the serial link: 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' (or equivalent) on the remote neighbor(s) connected to R2 Serial0/1/1 and R3 Serial0/1/1, or CDP/LLDP neighbor detail for those interfaces (to determine if the remote end changed state or is the cause).
If the serial link uses DCE/DTE cabling, confirm which side is DCE and provide the configured clock rate (or physical cable change history) for the link (to rule in/out missing clocking as a cause).

plan_history.intent_description: Two serial interfaces experienced state changes: router2 Serial0/1/1 moved to oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up; router3 Serial0/1/1 is admin=down and oper=down. Goal: restore both links to oper=up (or confirm deliberate admin shutdown) and clear lower-layer fault on router2.
plan_history.problem: router2 Serial0/1/1 is oper=lower-layer-down with admin up; router3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down causing oper down.
plan_history.fix_summary: Bring router3 Serial0/1/1 administratively up. Perform an admin bounce on router2 Serial0/1/1 to attempt to clear the lower-layer fault. These actions may cause a brief outage on the serial links.
